inline_review:
commit eee8183c7ff033cdc7c1e45c39064cf1d0099d4c
Author: wangzhen <wangzhen5@honor.com>
Subject: mm/vmscan: add tracepoint for changes in min_seq and max_seq

This commit adds two new tracepoints, mm_vmscan_lru_inc_min_seq and
mm_vmscan_lru_inc_max_seq, to provide finer granularity tracking of mglru
sequence changes compared to debugfs.

> diff --git a/include/trace/events/vmscan.h b/include/trace/events/vmscan.h
> index 4445a8d9218d..7c21ee1ed488 100644
> --- a/include/trace/events/vmscan.h
> +++ b/include/trace/events/vmscan.h
> @@ -509,6 +509,55 @@ TRACE_EVENT(mm_vmscan_lru_shrink_active,
>  		show_reclaim_flags(__entry->reclaim_flags))
>  );
>  
> +#ifdef CONFIG_LRU_GEN
> +TRACE_EVENT(mm_vmscan_lru_inc_min_seq,
> +
> +	TP_PROTO(struct mem_cgroup *memcg, int type, int swappiness, unsigned long min_seq),
> +
> +	TP_ARGS(memcg, type, swappiness, min_seq),
> +
> +	TP_STRUCT__entry(
> +		__field(int, id)

Since mem_cgroup_id() returns a u64, will storing it in a 32-bit signed int
truncate large cgroup IDs?

> +		__field(int, type)
> +		__field(int, swappiness)
> +		__field(unsigned long, min_seq)
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_fast_assign(
> +		__entry->id = mem_cgroup_id(memcg);
> +		__entry->type = type;
> +		__entry->swappiness = swappiness;
> +		__entry->min_seq = min_seq;
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_printk("memcg_id=%d type=%d swappiness=%d min_seq=%ld",

If the id field is updated to u64, this %d would also need to be updated
to %llu.

Also, min_seq is passed and stored as an unsigned long. Could formatting
it with %ld eventually result in negative values in the trace output?
Would %lu be a better fit here?

> +		__entry->id, __entry->type,
> +		__entry->swappiness, __entry->min_seq)
> +);
> +
> +TRACE_EVENT(mm_vmscan_lru_inc_max_seq,
> +
> +	TP_PROTO(struct mem_cgroup *memcg, int swappiness, unsigned long max_seq),
> +
> +	TP_ARGS(memcg, swappiness, max_seq),
> +
> +	TP_STRUCT__entry(
> +		__field(int, id)

Similar to the min_seq tracepoint, does this id need to be a u64?

> +		__field(int, swappiness)
> +		__field(unsigned long, max_seq)
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_fast_assign(
> +		__entry->id = mem_cgroup_id(memcg);
> +		__entry->swappiness = swappiness;
> +		__entry->max_seq = max_seq;
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_printk("memcg_id=%d swappiness=%d max_seq=%ld",

And should max_seq be printed with %lu instead of %ld to match its
unsigned long type?

> +		__entry->id, __entry->swappiness, __entry->max_seq)
> +);
> +#endif /* CONFIG_LRU_GEN */
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Truncation of 64-bit cgroup ID to 32-bit signed integer in tracepoints","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"1. The function `mem_cgroup_id(memcg)` returns a `u64` representing the cgroup ID.
2. In `TP_STRUCT__entry` for both `mm_vmscan_lru_inc_min_seq` and `mm_vmscan_lru_inc_max_seq`, the `id` field is declared as `int` (`__field(int, id)`).
3. In `TP_fast_assign`, the 64-bit value is assigned to this 32-bit field.
4. In `TP_printk`, it is printed using `%d`.
This silently truncates the 64-bit ID to 32 bits, which will result in incorrect cgroup IDs in the trace output on systems with large cgroup IDs (and will print as negative numbers if the ID exceeds 2^31-1). It should be defined as `u64` and printed with `%llu`."},{"problem":"Format specifier mismatch for unsigned long sequence numbers","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"1. The `min_seq` and `max_seq` arguments are correctly declared as `unsigned long` in both tracepoint prototypes and `TP_STRUCT__entry`.
2. However, their values are formatted in `TP_printk` using `%ld` (signed long) instead of `%lu` (unsigned long).
3. While MGLRU sequence numbers increment slowly and may take a long time to reach the sign bit, using a signed format specifier for an unsigned type is incorrect and can result in negative values being printed."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit eee8183c7ff033cdc7c1e45c39064cf1d0099d4c
Author: wangzhen <wangzhen5@honor.com>
Subject: mm/vmscan: add tracepoint for changes in min_seq and max_seq

This commit adds two new tracepoints, mm_vmscan_lru_inc_min_seq and
mm_vmscan_lru_inc_max_seq, to provide finer granularity tracking of mglru
sequence changes compared to debugfs.

> diff --git a/include/trace/events/vmscan.h b/include/trace/events/vmscan.h
> index 4445a8d9218d..7c21ee1ed488 100644
> --- a/include/trace/events/vmscan.h
> +++ b/include/trace/events/vmscan.h
> @@ -509,6 +509,55 @@ TRACE_EVENT(mm_vmscan_lru_shrink_active,
>  		show_reclaim_flags(__entry->reclaim_flags))
>  );
>  
> +#ifdef CONFIG_LRU_GEN
> +TRACE_EVENT(mm_vmscan_lru_inc_min_seq,
> +
> +	TP_PROTO(struct mem_cgroup *memcg, int type, int swappiness, unsigned long min_seq),
> +
> +	TP_ARGS(memcg, type, swappiness, min_seq),
> +
> +	TP_STRUCT__entry(
> +		__field(int, id)

Since mem_cgroup_id() returns a u64, will storing it in a 32-bit signed int
truncate large cgroup IDs?

> +		__field(int, type)
> +		__field(int, swappiness)
> +		__field(unsigned long, min_seq)
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_fast_assign(
> +		__entry->id = mem_cgroup_id(memcg);
> +		__entry->type = type;
> +		__entry->swappiness = swappiness;
> +		__entry->min_seq = min_seq;
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_printk("memcg_id=%d type=%d swappiness=%d min_seq=%ld",

If the id field is updated to u64, this %d would also need to be updated
to %llu.

Also, min_seq is passed and stored as an unsigned long. Could formatting
it with %ld eventually result in negative values in the trace output?
Would %lu be a better fit here?

> +		__entry->id, __entry->type,
> +		__entry->swappiness, __entry->min_seq)
> +);
> +
> +TRACE_EVENT(mm_vmscan_lru_inc_max_seq,
> +
> +	TP_PROTO(struct mem_cgroup *memcg, int swappiness, unsigned long max_seq),
> +
> +	TP_ARGS(memcg, swappiness, max_seq),
> +
> +	TP_STRUCT__entry(
> +		__field(int, id)

Similar to the min_seq tracepoint, does this id need to be a u64?

> +		__field(int, swappiness)
> +		__field(unsigned long, max_seq)
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_fast_assign(
> +		__entry->id = mem_cgroup_id(memcg);
> +		__entry->swappiness = swappiness;
> +		__entry->max_seq = max_seq;
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_printk("memcg_id=%d swappiness=%d max_seq=%ld",

And should max_seq be printed with %lu instead of %ld to match its
unsigned long type?

> +		__entry->id, __entry->swappiness, __entry->max_seq)
> +);
> +#endif /* CONFIG_LRU_GEN */

"}
